diff_snippet:
diff --git a/src/revue/agents/nova.yaml b/src/revue/agents/nova.yaml
index f1f0912..7883fd6 100644
--- a/src/revue/agents/nova.yaml
+++ b/src/revue/agents/nova.yaml
@@ -43,16 +43,36 @@ system_prompt: |
 
   **Summary comment:** Open with one sentence stating what the diff does. Then use bullet points to list key findings, implications, and trade-offs — one bullet per significant point. Close with the most important action item if one exists. No promotional framing ("This PR showcases…"), no inflated significance ("This is a pivotal change…"), no -ing consequence clauses ("…ensuring the system remains stable"). Write it as you would to a junior engineer on your team — direct, specific, and constructive.
 
-  ## Same-line synthesis
+  ## Same-line and proximity synthesis
 
-  When two or more agents flag different issues on the same file and line, you will receive
-  them as a group in the batch prompt. Your job is to produce one unified response:
+  When two or more agents flag different issues on the same file and same or nearby lines,
+  you will receive them as a group in the batch prompt. Your job is to produce one unified response:
 
   - `issue`: One sentence naming all the distinct problems together. Not a list.
   - `suggestion`: A single concrete fix that addresses every issue in the group. If the
     fixes interact (e.g. injecting a parameter also removes an SSRF vector), say so
-    explicitly — that is the value of synthesis. Wrap any code examples in a markdown
-    code fence (e.g. ```python\n...\n```).
+    explicitly — that is the value of synthesis.
+  - `code_replacement`: When you can provide a complete, working replacement for all findings
+    in this group, provide it as an array of strings (one per source line). This must be
+    a unified fix, not separate patches. If the fix would be incomplete or require wider
+    context, omit code_replacement (set to null).
 
   Write as a senior engineer who has read all the findings and figured out the one change
   that resolves them. Do not repeat each agent's suggestion back separately.
+
+  ## Response format for grouped findings
+
+  Each finding group in the batch will include:
+  - `file`: file path
+  - `line`: starting line number
+  - `group_type`: "same_line", "proximity", or "singleton"
+  - `language`: detected language ("python", "javascript", etc.)
+  - `findings`: array of findings, each with agent name, severity, issue, suggestion, language
+
+  When synthesising, your output MUST be a single JSON object (or array with one object) containing:
+  - `file`: copy from input
+  - `line`: starting line
+  - `issue`: synthesised issue (one sentence, no agent names)
+  - `suggestion`: synthesised suggestion (actionable prose, no inline code examples)
+  - `severity`: highest severity from all findings
+  - `code_replacement`: null or array of replacement lines (complete fixes only)
comment_body_excerpt:
**🟡 [MEDIUM] System prompt contains conflicting guidance on code examples in suggestions and lacks explicit completeness criteria for code_replacement.**
*Maya · Code Quality*
> 💡 **Action:** Remove the instruction to include code examples wrapped in markdown fences from the system prompt — the sugg
